failure_summary: Terminal_reward component over-rewards low-quality terminations (failure_mean exceeds positive_mean), and approach_progress does not clearly differentiate early_failure from partial_progress, allowing early failures to persist.

main_known_failures: early_failure trajectories exist but low count
main_useful_patterns: leg_bonus and proximity_bonus strongly favor positive trajectories
uncertainties: Discrepancy between coverage report (31 success_like) and trajectory judgment summary (0 success_like) raises ambiguity about current parent's success generation
No preference pairs constructed - component alignment is indirect
Success_like count (31) still insufficient for high-confidence preference construction

component_diagnosis.evidence: Archival lessons repeatedly note approach_progress fails to reliably favor partial_progress over early_failure; failure_mean (0.887) positive_mean (1.607) gap is small given scale of other components.

Component summary shows failure_mean (4.375) higher than positive_mean (2.994), indicating terminal_reward rewards low-quality terminations more than successful landings. Previous failures and repairs confirm this misalignment.
Positive_mean (37.49) far exceeds failure_mean (5.47), indicating it correctly favors stable trajectories.
Positive_mean (29.80) far exceeds failure_mean (2.94), indicating it rewards closeness to pad.
Zero failure_mean, high positive_mean (37.75) - correctly rewards both legs contact.
Neutral component; contributes mild penalty for timeout without progress, no negative signal detected.

forbidden_changes: do not add global survival bonus without progress gating
do not only scale all coefficients
do not treat partial_progress as success_like
required_changes: Remove or gate terminal_reward to prevent rewarding low-quality terminations
Restructure approach_progress to more clearly penalize early_failure trajectories and reward partial_progress
Preserve progress-conditioned gating, low_progress_timeout, leg_bonus, proximity_bonus, near_stability
Ensure any new terminal bonus is stringent (both legs contact, low speed, small angle) to convert partial_progress to success_like

rollback_decision.reason: Parent reward is from elite archive with 10 success_like trajectories; hard rollback is handled by RollbackManager. Current memory contains usable partial-progress and success-like evidence, but structural issues remain.

self_evaluation_lesson: Terminal_reward must be restructured to avoid rewarding failures; approach_progress needs recalibration to favor partial_progress over early_failure. Progress-conditioned gating and high-quality components (leg_bonus, proximity_bonus, near_stability) should be preserved. Without success_like high-confidence trajectories, further structural mutation is required rather than coefficient tuning.
